story.title: Dr. Chen: Solving the Atomic Puzzle
story.text:
Hello, I am Dr. Sarah Chen. I study why materials act the way they do. My job is like solving a puzzle at the atomic level. Let's look at gold and iron. They are made of the same basic particles. So why are they so different? The answer is in their Atomic Number. This number tells us how many protons are in an atom.

Gold has 79 protons. Iron has only 26. The number of protons decides the Electron Configuration. This is how electrons are arranged in shells. The arrangement of the outer electrons is very important. It controls the element's Reactivity. Iron has outer electrons that react easily with oxygen. This is why iron rusts. Gold's outer electrons are very stable. Gold does not react easily.

This electron arrangement also affects Conductivity. Both gold and iron are good conductors. Their electrons can move freely. Finally, all these tiny details create the big Material Properties we see. We see gold's shine and iron's rust. My work helps us choose the right element for every job.
think_abouts: Why is gold a good material for jewelry?
What happens to an iron nail if you leave it outside in the rain?
story.go_deeper: If a new element was discovered with a very stable electron configuration, would you expect it to be more like gold or more like iron? Why?

margin_defs.definition: not likely to change or react
the energy levels where electrons are found

check_prompts: What part of an atom determines how it will react with other elements?

vocabulary.definition: The number of protons in an atom's center. It identifies the element.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The atomic number of carbon is 6 because it has 6 protons.

vocabulary.definition: The way electrons are arranged in levels or shells around an atom's center.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The electron configuration helps us predict how an element will behave.

vocabulary.definition: How likely an element is to have a chemical reaction with other things.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Because of its high reactivity, sodium reacts strongly with water.

vocabulary.definition: The ability of a material to let electricity or heat pass through it.
vocabulary.use_sentence: Copper has high conductivity, so we use it for electrical wires.

vocabulary.definition: The features you can see or measure, like color, hardness, or shininess.
vocabulary.use_sentence: The material properties of diamonds make them hard and sparkly.

discussion_cards.prompt: Dr. Chen says her work helps us choose the right element for a job. What is a job where you would choose gold? What is a job where you would choose iron?
discussion_cards.frame: For the job of ___, I would choose gold because ___. For the job of ___, I would choose iron because ___.

discussion_cards.prompt: Why is it important for scientists like Dr. Chen to understand how even tiny particles like electrons affect the world we see?
discussion_cards.frame: It is important for scientists to understand tiny particles because ___.

reflection_frames.knew: Before reading, I already knew that ___
reflection_frames.learned: From the story, I learned that ___
